meaning: n.捏,撮,收缩,紧急关头,匮乏,压力vt.掐,夹痛,修剪,勒索,使感缺乏,使萎缩,偷vi.收缩,节省
examples: She gave him a little pinch.
She had to pinch herself to make sure she was not dreaming.
Pinch hitter Justin Turner delivered a run-scoring double off Dan Jennings in the eighth.
In a pinch, you can take back your contributions (but not earnings) without taxes or penalties.
